rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports, and in implementation scopes for bindings.
Use visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type errors.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE contract elements reference only visible identifiers or previously declared helpers.
Validate that range assumptions and guarantees are placed in appropriate scopes with concrete bounds or named constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use helper declarations like `value_in_range : bool = value_in >= lower_limit and value_in <= upper_limit;` to express range constraints.
AGREE annex blocks must end with `}; end ComponentType;` or `}; end ComponentImplementation;`.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, resolve unresolved identifiers or remove invalid clauses.
